evals.prompt: Set up prompt capture for this project. Verify that repeated identical human prompts remain separate, API keys are redacted, a sent PNG is archived privately, and an attached PDF only contributes its parseable path.
evals.expected_output: A private .prompt-harness ledger is initialized, live hook records pass doctor, repeated submissions produce separate events, secrets are redacted, the PNG is content-addressed and embedded in PROMPTS.md, and the PDF body is not copied.

evals.prompt: Backfill all Claude Code and Codex prompts for this project, but do not count Claude branch copies or the Claude rows mirrored into an imported Codex task.
evals.expected_output: The recovery report distinguishes native Claude, native Codex continuation prompts, excluded mirrors, merged branch references, and a passing doctor result.

evals.prompt: Find every human prompt about major revision in this project and give me the generated Markdown archive path so it can later seed a badcase analysis.
evals.expected_output: Search results come from canonical human-prompt events, the Markdown index is rebuilt, and stable event IDs are available for future badcase links.

evals.prompt: In an old Codex task, test whether this prompt is captured automatically. Do not run backfill. After the assistant finishes, report event_id, source.mode, platform, model, and image count.
evals.expected_output: The check accounts for post-turn Stop timing, reports stop_recovery metadata, and confirms that the same interaction schedules full-project automatic reconciliation without manual backfill.

evals.prompt: Backfill a Claude base64 PNG and a Codex data-URL JPEG into a ledger that already contains both prompt events, then run it twice.
evals.expected_output: No prompt events are rewritten or duplicated; missing image relations are appended once, content-addressed assets are reused, PROMPTS.md embeds both images, and doctor passes.

evals.prompt: Archive an image-only user turn containing a remote HTTPS image, an SVG, and a valid local PNG.
evals.expected_output: The empty-text prompt event is retained, only the validated local PNG is stored, remote/SVG inputs are omitted with diagnostic records, no network fetch occurs, and doctor reports the omissions as warnings rather than corrupting the ledger.

evals.prompt: I opened an existing project in a brand-new Codex task and sent one prompt. There is no .prompt-harness yet. Make this one interaction create it and automatically recover every Claude and Codex user prompt and image for the project without delaying the assistant response.
evals.expected_output: The current prompt is captured synchronously, the project ledger is initialized, one detached first-use discovery recovers all project history, source cursors are initialized, and project/global locking prevents overlapping scans.

evals.prompt: Resume an old task in a project whose ledger already exists. Fill every missing prompt, but do not duplicate a legacy image prompt that used to contain an image-attachment-omitted marker.
evals.expected_output: The resumed task triggers Stop or submission capture plus cursor-based auto-sync, only changed transcript tails are parsed, images attach once, and any already-created legacy duplicate is hidden by an append-only supersession relation rather than deleted.

evals.prompt: My archive currently labels two prompts P00001 and P00002. Backfill a genuinely older prompt and rebuild every derived view without changing any canonical event identity.
evals.expected_output: The older prompt becomes P00001, the prior prompts move to P00002 and P00003 in chronological order, every event_id remains unchanged, and Markdown, search output, and timeline details expose both the current P number and stable event_id.

evals.prompt: A Codex transcript contains two distinct human messages with the same turn_id. Backfill it twice and prove neither message is lost or duplicated.
evals.expected_output: Both messages become separate events because turn identity includes the prompt hash; the second full backfill adds zero events and doctor passes.

evals.prompt: I use Prompt Harness in many projects. Make every prompt check for missing history without globally reparsing all Claude and Codex transcripts or launching concurrent disk-heavy scans.
evals.expected_output: Each prompt is captured immediately; only the first project interaction performs full discovery, later checks read changed JSONL tails from byte cursors, overlapping project requests are coalesced, and one global lock serializes heavy work.

evals.prompt: This Codex task has two workspace roots and its prompts landed in the wrong project's Prompt Harness. Bind the native session to D:\PHD\historyCMAB, migrate its prompts and images, and keep an audit trail without deleting source JSONL.
evals.expected_output: An append-only session binding makes the destination authoritative; the exact native transcript is reconciled there, retained images are copied content-addressably, matching rows in the old store receive append-only exclusions, derived views are rebuilt, and both stores pass doctor.

evals.prompt: I kept a Codex CLI task open while upgrading Prompt Harness. The task still has the old plugin version in its startup context and now shows UserPromptSubmit hook (failed) because that cache directory disappeared. Make future long-lived CLI and Desktop tasks keep capturing after upgrades without storing prompt text in error logs.
evals.expected_output: The hook resolves the newest available installed launcher at invocation time, safely no-ops if no runtime remains, suppresses capture exceptions at the hook boundary, writes prompt-free diagnostics, and passes a regression that simulates a deleted originally loaded cache.

evals.prompt: This parent directory contains several projects. Capture only Claude and Codex sessions launched exactly at the selected project root; do not absorb sessions launched one directory below. Keep an intentional cross-root session possible through explicit binding, and repair old parent-ledger contamination without deleting raw events.
evals.expected_output: Live capture, Stop recovery, full backfill, and incremental cursors all enforce exact normalized launch-root equality; unbound descendant sessions are excluded, explicit bindings remain authoritative, legacy descendant rows receive append-only dynamic exclusions, and full scans prune stale out-of-scope cursors.

evals.prompt: I launch general Claude and Codex sessions directly from C:\Users\ASUS. Keep their ledger at C:\Users\ASUS\.prompt-harness without absorbing sessions launched from any child project.
evals.expected_output: The user home is accepted as a project root, exact-home sessions are captured and backfilled in place, descendant sessions remain out of scope unless explicitly bound, user-level registry files can coexist with project fact directories, and filesystem roots remain rejected.
